response.global_summary: The video is a live performance by the VTuber Matsuri Lui (魔鎖ルイ) singing the song 'アイのシナリオ' (Ai no Scenario) by HoneyWorks. The performance features dynamic lighting changes and stage transitions as she sings the popular song.

scenes.description: Matsuri Lui performs the song on stage under intense red lighting, transitioning to purple.
scenes.reference_lyrics_og:
世界欺く揺るぎない正義
ほどいて真実この手の中

大胆不敵な影が華麗に踊る
君が隠してる秘密いつか聞かせてよ
伸ばした手で触れる事は誰もできない
君が隠してる世界僕に見させてよ

揺れる難解な心
解き明かして奪うその瞳
不安がって泣いてたって見つけらんないよ
狙う真実はどこへ消えた

たとえ
世界欺く解答（こたえ）だとしても
信じて差し出す掌
決して逃げない怖くは無いから
目を開け弱さをかき消すんだ

繰り返される運命に君は気づいてる？
僕が隠してる秘密いつか聞かせるよ
歩み寄る偽りの影君は知らない
芽生えた記憶は絡む必然の棘で

守りたい想いでただ
立ち向かって解（ほど）くその絆
何度だって助けるって揺るぎはしないよ
隠す真実はどこへ消えた

たとえ
世界を敵に回したとしても
「信じて」僕だけ未来も
決して逃げない迷いは無いから
シナリオ壊して抗うんだ

世界を敵に回したとしても
「信じて」差し出す掌
決して離さない想い（こたえ）は届いた
結んだ運命この手の中

繰り返された解答（こたえ）だとしても
決して逃げない怖くは無いから
抗うんだ
scenes.reference_lyrics_en:
The world is once again deceived, by justice, unwavering
and as the truth unravels now
in these hands you held out
a daring shadow dancing gracefully
disappeared without a trace
and this little secret that you hid from me
could you tell me it, someday?
held out your hand, but nobody could reach
it was just too far away
and this little world that you hid from me
could you show me it, someday?
as your heart begins to waver and obscure
those eyes, with what they saw stole it all, leaving nothing more
even if you cry, or wonder why, still the answer escapes you
and the truth that i’ve been searching for had vanished too!
even if
the world is once again deceived, by an answer that you see
i’ll take again, your outstretched hand, i will hold on!
and i will never run away, i’ll no longer be afraid
open your eyes just one more time, all your fears will be gone
never ending loop, that trapped us two
did you notice it already?
and this little secret that i hid from you
could i tell you it, someday?
and this little shadow that was battered and worn
it was fake, that’s what they thought
memories of yours, tangled up in thorns
awaken with just a touch
i’ll protect these memories that i hold dear
fighting against these bonds, i’ll go on, i will be right here!
if you cry out, if you need help, just ask i’ll be there for you
and the truth that i’ve been searching for had vanished too!
even if
the world has turned on you and me, if they’re all the enemy
this future that i wanted back, i won’t give up!
and i will never run away, i’ll no longer lose my way
scenario, love that we know. i will keep fighting on!
the world has turned on you and me, if they’re all the enemy
i’ll take again your outstretched hand, until the end
this answer that you need to see, has it reached you finally?
this binding fate that ties us down
in these hand you held out
this answer that i’ve heard before, could it mean something else more?
and i will never run away, i’ll no longer be afraid
i will keep fighting on!

scenes.description: The performance continues, shifting to white and blue stage lighting.

response.thoughts:
**My Analysis of the VTuber Performance Video**

Okay, so I've got a video of a VTuber performance to analyze. The prompt says it's a cover, so I need to identify the song, artist, and performer, and map out the video's structure.

First, I need to look at the video. It's a continuous performance on what appears to be a stage. There's on-screen text, which is key. I see "アイのシナリオ" – that's the song title. And right below it is the creator, “HoneyWorks”. This is good, a solid start. I'll need to break the video down into scenes, and it looks like I can do this based on lighting or camera angle changes. The prompt wants "chronological, non-overlapping scenes mapping the entire video segment," so that is the direction I will go in.

Now, let's get down to the details. The video jumps right into the song at 00:00.

I need to nail down the VTuber's identity. Wait...on-screen, in the corner, I see "#魔鎖ルイ". That's Matsuri Lui, and so that's who's performing.

Next, I'll need to locate lyrics. I'll do a quick search using the song title "アイのシナリオ", the artist "HoneyWorks", and also the keywords “lyrics” and “English” to see what I can turn up.

Finally, I'll structure the data as JSON, and begin building a draft. My `global_summary` will state that the video is a live performance by Matsuri Lui (confirmed!). The `scenes` will be based on the visual segments I identified. I'll mark `contains_vocal_music` as `True` for all scenes, as the video is a song, and I'll include the `song_title` ("アイのシナリオ"), the `original_artist` ("HoneyWorks"), the `performer_in_video` ("Matsuri Lui"), and the `original_language` ("Japanese"). I'll need to be careful not to truncate any lyrics if I happen to find them. I'll copy the whole thing.

Okay, let's get to searching.



**My Analysis of "Ai no Scenario" Performance by Matsuri Lui**

Okay, so I've got the Japanese lyrics and a solid English translation for "Ai no Scenario" by HoneyWorks, performed by Matsuri Lui. Now I'm structuring the breakdown of the performance, thinking about how to map the visual progression against the song's structure. Initially, I was breaking it down a bit granularly, but on second thought, it's better to group things based on the flow of the song and what I observe in the video itself. I'm aiming for a more logical grouping that feels natural and avoids overly granular segments.

I realized I can refine the scene divisions further by taking into account visual cues, like changes in lighting or camera angles, to give a more accurate representation of the performance's flow. Based on the video, the performance can be divided into six scenes. The first sets the tone with red lighting, then progresses with white/pink lighting, a view of the crowd and stage, a dynamic dance/instrumental section, the continued performance, and finally a conclusive climax.

I'm confident I have everything I need now. I'll make sure to use the full lyrics, and since the prompt states "no manual transcription", I will use the verified online versions. I have the Japanese lyrics ready, and the English translation is also there. That should be a solid framework for the JSON output.
